clean_body: I want to hear from you | Friend, I’m not the first to say it, and I probably won’t be the last - I’m tired of President “Hidin’” Biden. I’m tired of a “President” who **talks about his favorite ice cream more than he talks to the American people. I’m tired of a “President” who does nothing about skyrocketing prices and widespread inflation, even as everyday Americans like you and me are suffering. I’m tired of a “President” who is not respected on the world stage and constantly embarrasses our great nation. And I’m tired of Abigail Spanberger passively putting up with him and NOT FIGHTING BACK. Let me know - I’m curious to hear how you feel.** Thank you, Yesli Vega Conservative Republican for Congress Virginia's 7th District | Paid for by Yesli Vega for Virginia Don't want to receive our emails anymore?

# Greetings From the Campaign Trail!
---
| | |
---|---
|
Being in the middle of caucus season, we have been busier than ever in our efforts to get the message of freedom out to the many voters of the 2nd District!

In addition to the county caucuses in Rock, Green, and Iowa Counties so far in February, our team has been keeping busy and packing the miles on the cars going to great events like the CareNet fundraising dinner in Monroe, the FAIR Conference in Madison (a great non-partisan group to counteract Critical Theory in our schools and workplace), Patriot Group meetings in Reedsburg, Pints & Politics in Waunakee, a luncheon with Election Integrity Champion Janel Brandtjen in Maple Bluff, and so much more!

Also, this week, we released our first two ads on Facebook! The word is getting out - we have had great responses, more people following our campaign, and even signing up to volunteer because of these ads!
| |
---|---
|
Our message of hope, enthusiasm, and confidence that the people of District 2 don't have to settle for the destructive policies of Mark Pocan just because it's not an easy seat to win is a message that resonates with the voters! We are all ready for new and accurate representation, and we can get it if we work hard, implementing both old and new campaign techniques and leaving no rock unturned. Which is why this is just the start of our digital ad campaign.

As my team and I look forward to all the things ahead of us in the coming weeks, we are excited for the opportunities in store. But to keep the campaign running, we need your help! Please consider a donation today to help us get the word out - meetings take gas money, literature takes printing money, ads take dispersal money. GETTING THE WORD OUT TAKES A TEAM!!!

Thank you so much!

Charity Barry

name: Team Erica Smith
subject: Erica’s latest endorsement >>
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Erica Smith for US Senate 2022
body:
[1]Erica Smith

Our
mid-month fundraising deadline is hours away — but before we get to that,
we have big news to share with you:

Sunrise Movement North Carolina just endorsed our campaign for NC-02!

Sunrise Movement North Carolina is a youth-led coalition fighting to make
climate change an urgent priority, end the corrupting influence of fossil
fuel executives on our politics, and elect leaders who stand up for the
health and wellbeing of all people in North Carolina and beyond.

Erica is proud to receive this endorsement, and she’ll fight tirelessly to
pass climate change legislation in Congress — but we’re counting on your
help to get her there.

Our mid-month fundraising deadline is TONIGHT, and we’re still $565 away
from reaching our $20,000 goal. That’s why we’re asking: [ https://act.ericaforus.com/go/40403?refcode=em20220214-877&t=1001&akid=877%2E3698%2EDKYSCm ]Will you make
a contribution to our campaign to help us close the gap and send Erica to
Congress to fight for critical climate policies?

[ https://act.ericaforus.com/go/40403?refcode=em20220214-877&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=877%2E3698%2EDKYSCm ]Donate $5

From flooding in Princeville to tornadoes in Bertie, we’ve witnessed
firsthand the devastation caused by the climate emergency in eastern North
Carolina. There’s no more time left for inaction.

That’s why in Congress, Erica will fight against corporate polluters that
use their dirty money to corrupt our political system and be an advocate
for bold climate action.

But she can’t do this alone. Erica doesn’t come from wealth, and she
refuses to take a dime of corporate PAC or fossil fuel money. That means
it’ll take a lot of people power to compete against our corporate-backed
opponents.

[ https://act.ericaforus.com/go/40403?refcode=em20220214-877&t=1021&akid=877%2E3698%2EDKYSCm ]That’s why your donation today is so important. Can you chip in right
now so we can close our $565 fundraising gap before midnight and build a
powerful movement to send Erica to the halls of Congress?

Thank you,

Team Erica Smith

Contribute to Erica's campaign:https://secure.actblue.com/donate/es-emails?refcode=em20220214-877-footer&t=1022&refcode2=877_3698_DKYSCm&akid=877%2E3698%2EDKYSCm

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Erica for Us, P.O. Box 1236, Gaston, NC 27832.

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.ericaforus.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=877%2E3698%2EDKYSCm

PAID FOR BY Erica Smith for US Senate 2022

But she can’t do this alone. Erica doesn’t come from wealth, and she refuses to take a dime of corporate PAC or fossil fuel money. That means it’ll take a lot of people power to compete against our corporate-backed opponents. [ ]That’s why your donation today is so important. Can you chip in right now so we can close our $565 fundraising gap before midnight and build a powerful movement to send Erica to the halls of Congress? Thank you, Team Erica Smith Contribute to Erica's campaign:

---
| 2022 The Year the Republican Party Platform Died in Mississippi
---
---
| | |
---
| Mississippi Republican Party - Dead?
---
**Jackson, MS (February 14, 2022) —** If you’ve gone looking for the MS GOP party platform lately, you’ll have to do some digging to find it. The platform was removed from the Republican Party’s website, although the historic framework can still be found empty on the webpage, and if you dig far enough using an internet archive system such as “Wayback Machine,” you can pull the now archived party platform. The platform was last adopted May 14, 2016, and at the time the Party “fundamentally believe[d] in the conservative principles of individual freedom, personal responsibility, free market economic policies, and a society that respects our God given rights of life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness.” One of the main tenets of the platform included “Integrity in Elections” which stated, “the integrity of our election process should be vigilantly protected and improved by our government” and that “we support efforts to restore and maintain the integrity of our voter registration rolls.”
Those points were mainstays of the Republican Party and of conservatives everywhere, but they are slowly losing ground in MS. The MS GOP has pulled the platform from their website and Leaders in the State House of Representatives appear to side with the minority party of Democrats when it comes to election integrity. As an article late last week in the Clarion Ledger headlined, [“](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xODg1ODI0NDgyODc1MDIxOTc1JmM9ejV6MyZlPTAmYj05MDg1OTkzMTkmZD1hMG4xcTli.E_8SAP1_QoBskbnNs12jOmMkPHwk0AHHqBKXGOrHFEI)**[House Democrats get rare win as divided Republicans approve diminished election bill.”](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xODg1ODI0NDgyODc1MDIxOTc1JmM9ejV6MyZlPTAmYj05MDg1OTkzMjUmZD1jOW81bzF0.dGP2gDryL-N-QutDiVBNfyTJ0PfgtDzCBWs1ZYcmRWE)**
Why may you ask, should the Democrats get a win on a topic that is the cornerstone of our Republic, in stripping an election integrity bill down to almost nothing, when the Democrats only represent a paltry 40% of the chamber? It certainly wasn’t only the Democrats at the helm of steering this sinking ship. The strike-all amendment that did the dirty deed of making a once decent bill into something weak and lackluster, was presented by a Republican. Moments after the strike-all bill was introduced, a Democrat introduced an amendment that would make it even harder to purge voter rolls. This all transpired, have you, under the careful guise of the Republican House Leadership. The Democrat House Minority Leader Robert Johnson indicated after the vote, that the Republicans have been more willing to compromise this session than in the past. Why are Republicans compromising? Republicans make up a super-majority in both legislative chambers and yet what do we have to show for it?
The Mississippi Freedom Caucus (MFC) members in the meantime are called “far-right,” “hyper-conservative,” and “super-conservative” in the same Clarion Ledger article. Is the MFC hyper-conservative for following what was a tenant of the Republican Party just a year ago? Maybe we are, or maybe the Republican Leaders in the MS House of Representatives are letting the party platform go to the wayside and are aligning with the Democrats more than members of their own party.
Now the “election integrity” bill is on its way to the MS State Senate, where we can only hope that the true conservatives on that side of the building can prove to the rest of us in Mississippi that the tenants of the old GOP aren’t yet dead. If not, 2022 may indeed be marked as the year that the Republican Party platform died in Mississippi.
_###_

Moments after the strike-all bill was introduced, a Democrat introduced an amendment that would make it even harder to purge voter rolls. This all transpired, have you, under the careful guise of the Republican House Leadership. The Democrat House Minority Leader Robert Johnson indicated after the vote, that the Republicans have been more willing to compromise this session than in the past. Why are Republicans compromising? Republicans make up a super-majority in both legislative chambers and yet what do we have to show for it? The Mississippi Freedom Caucus (MFC) members in the meantime are called “far-right,” “hyper-conservative,” and “super-conservative” in the same Clarion Ledger article. Is the MFC hyper-conservative for following what was a tenant of the Republican Party just a year ago? Maybe we are, or maybe the Republican Leaders in the MS House of Representatives are letting the party platform go to the wayside and are aligning with the Democrats more than members of their own party. Now the “election integrity” bill is on its way to the MS State Senate, where we can only hope that the true conservatives on that side of the building can prove to the rest of us in Mississippi that the tenants of the old GOP aren’t yet dead.
